34	    BaseGrammarObject,
35	    GrammarStats,
36	)
37	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag
38	from sglang.srt.utils import is_hip
39	
40	_is_hip = is_hip()
41	if _is_hip:
42	    from sgl_kernel import apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda
43	else:
44	    from sglang.srt.constrained.triton_ops.bitmask_ops import (
45	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton,
46	    )
47	
48	
49	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
50	MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS = 200
51	
52	
53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
101	
102	    @staticmethod
103	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
104	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
105	
106	    def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
107	        if (
108	            logits.device.type == "cuda"
109	            or logits.device.type == "npu"
110	            or logits.device.type == "xpu"
111	        ):
112	            if _is_hip:
113	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda(logits, vocab_mask)
114	            else:
115	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
116	        elif logits.device.type == "cpu" and self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu:
117	            self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu(logits, vocab_mask)
118	        else:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported device: {logits.device.type}")
120	
121	    def copy(self):
122	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
123	            self.ctx,
124	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
125	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
126	        )
127	        return XGrammarGrammar(
128	            matcher,
129	            self.vocab_size,
130	            self.ctx,
131	            self.override_stop_tokens,
132	            self.key_string,
133	            dataclasses.replace(
134	                self.grammar_stats, is_cache_hit=True, tree_traversal_time=[]
135	            ),
136	        )
137	
138	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
139	        s = self.matcher.find_jump_forward_string()
140	        if s:
141	            return [], s
142	        return None
143	
144	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
145	        _, data = helper
146	        return data, -1
147	
148	    def jump_and_retokenize(
149	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
150	    ):
151	        k = 0
152	        for i, old_id in enumerate(old_output_ids):
153	            if old_id == new_output_ids[i]:
154	                k = i + 1
155	            else:
156	                break
157	
158	        # rollback to the last token that is the same
159	        if k < len(old_output_ids):
160	            self.matcher.rollback(len(old_output_ids) - k)
161	
162	        for i in range(k, len(new_output_ids)):
163	            assert self.matcher.accept_token(new_output_ids[i])
164	
165	    def __repr__(self):
166	        return f"XGrammarGrammar({self.key_string=}, {self.accepted_tokens=}, {self.current_token=})"
167	
168	
169	class TokenizerNotSupportedError(Exception):
170	    """Raised when tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar backend."""
171	
172	    pass
173	
174	
175	class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        tokenizer,
179	        vocab_size: int,
180	        model_eos_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
181	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
182	    ):
183	        super().__init__()
184	
185	        if hasattr(tokenizer, "init_xgrammar"):
186	            # For special tokenizer
187	            tokenizer_info, override_stop_tokens = tokenizer.init_xgrammar()
188	
189	            if tokenizer_info is None:
190	                # Not supported tokenizer
191	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
192	                    f"Tokenizer type {type(tokenizer).__name__} is not supported by XGrammar"
193	                )
194	        else:
195	            # Create TokenizerInfo with model's EOS tokens as the authoritative stop tokens
196	            # This ensures consistency between what the model considers EOS and what XGrammar uses
197	            try:
198	                tokenizer_info = TokenizerInfo.from_huggingface(
199	                    tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, stop_token_ids=model_eos_token_ids
200	                )

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())
55	
56	
57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state
91	
92	
93	def run_program_batch(
94	    program,
95	    backend,
96	    batch_arguments,
97	    default_sampling_para,
98	    num_threads,
99	    progress_bar,
100	    generator_style=False,
101	):
102	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
103	        backend = backend.endpoint
104	
105	    # Pre-cache the common prefix for a batch. The prefix is extracted by tracing the program.
106	    if global_config.enable_precache_with_tracing and len(batch_arguments) > 1:
107	        cache_program(program, backend)
108	
109	    # Run all programs
110	    if num_threads == "auto":
111	        num_threads = max(96, multiprocessing.cpu_count() * 16)
112	    num_threads = min(num_threads, len(batch_arguments))
113	
114	    if generator_style:
115	        return _run_program_batch_generator(
116	            program,
117	            backend,
118	            batch_arguments,
119	            default_sampling_para,
120	            num_threads,
121	            progress_bar,
122	        )
123	
124	    # Original code path when generator_style=False
125	    if num_threads == 1:
126	        rets = []
127	        if progress_bar:
128	            for arguments in tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments):
129	                rets.append(
130	                    run_program(
131	                        program,
132	                        backend,
133	                        (),
134	                        arguments,
135	                        default_sampling_para,
136	                        False,
137	                        True,
138	                    )
139	                )
140	        else:
141	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
142	                rets.append(
143	                    run_program(
144	                        program,
145	                        backend,
146	                        (),
147	                        arguments,
148	                        default_sampling_para,
149	                        False,
150	                        True,
151	                    )
152	                )
153	    else:
154	        if progress_bar:
155	            pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments))
156	
157	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
158	            futures = []
159	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
160	                futures.append(
161	                    executor.submit(
162	                        run_program,
163	                        program,
164	                        backend,
165	                        (),
166	                        arguments,
167	                        default_sampling_para,
168	                        False,
169	                        True,
170	                    )
171	                )
172	                if progress_bar:
173	                    futures[-1].add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
174	
175	            rets = [f.result() for f in futures]
176	        rets[-1].sync()
177	
178	        if progress_bar:
179	            pbar.close()
180	
181	    return rets
182	
183	
184	def _run_program_batch_generator(
185	    program,
186	    backend,
187	    batch_arguments,
188	    default_sampling_para,
189	    num_threads,
190	    progress_bar,
191	):
192	    """Helper function that yields results one by one using chunking to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor."""
193	    if num_threads == 1:
194	        iterator = tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments) if progress_bar else batch_arguments
195	        for arguments in iterator:
196	            yield run_program(
197	                program,
198	                backend,
199	                (),
200	                arguments,

127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
158	
159	    def generate(
160	        self,
161	        s: StreamExecutor,
162	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
163	    ):
164	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
165	        data = {
166	            "text": s.text_,
167	            "sampling_params": {
168	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
169	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
170	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
171	            },
172	        }
173	
174	        for item in [
175	            "return_logprob",
176	            "logprob_start_len",
177	            "top_logprobs_num",
178	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
179	        ]:
180	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
181	            if value is not None:
182	                data[item] = value
183	
184	        self._add_images(s, data)
185	
186	        res = http_request(
187	            self.base_url + "/generate",
188	            json=data,
189	            api_key=self.api_key,
190	            verify=self.verify,
191	        )
192	        self._assert_success(res)
193	
194	        obj = res.json()
195	        comp = obj["text"]
196	        return comp, obj["meta_info"]
197	
198	    def generate_stream(
199	        self,
200	        s: StreamExecutor,

202	    ):
203	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
204	
205	        data = {

209	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
210	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),

212	        }
213	
214	        for item in [
215	            "return_logprob",
216	            "logprob_start_len",
217	            "top_logprobs_num",
218	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
219	        ]:
220	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)

223	
224	        data["stream"] = True

227	        res = http_request(

229	            json=data,

231	            api_key=self.api_key,

233	        )
234	        self._assert_success(res)
235	        pos = 0
236	
237	        for chunk in res.iter_lines(decode_unicode=False):
238	            chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
239	            if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):

242	                data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
243	                chunk_text = data["text"][pos:]
244	                meta_info = data["meta_info"]

7	    def _run_decode_regex(
8	        self,
9	        regex,
10	        prompt,
11	        return_logprob=False,
12	        top_logprobs_num=0,
13	        n=1,
14	    ):
15	        response = requests.post(
16	            self.base_url + "/generate",
17	            json={
18	                "text": prompt,
19	                "sampling_params": {
20	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
21	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
22	                    "n": n,
23	                    "regex": regex,
24	                },
25	                "stream": False,
26	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
27	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
28	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
29	            },
30	        )
31	
32	        ret = response.json()
33	        print(json.dumps(ret, indent=2))
34	        print("=" * 100)
35	
36	        if not isinstance(ret, list):
37	            self.fail(f"Expected response to be a list, but got {type(ret)}")
38	
39	        for item in ret:
40	            text = item.get("text", "").strip()
41	            if not text:
42	                self.fail("Generated text is empty.")
43	
44	            if not self.regex_match(text, regex):
45	                self.fail(f"Text '{text}' does not match regex pattern.")
46	
47	    def regex_match(self, text, pattern):
48	        import re
49	
50	        return re.match(pattern, text) is not None
51	
52	    def test_regex_generate_email(self):
53	        pattern = r"^user@example\.com$"
54	        prompt = "Generate an email address:"
55	
56	        self._run_decode_regex(
57	            regex=pattern,
58	            prompt=prompt,
59	            n=3,
60	        )
61	
62	    def test_regex_generate_greeting(self):
63	        pattern = r"^(Hello|Hi|Hey)$"
64	        prompt = "Generate a greeting:"
65	
66	        self._run_decode_regex(
67	            regex=pattern,
68	            prompt=prompt,
69	            n=3,
70	        )
71	
72	    def test_regex_generate_number(self):
73	        pattern = r"^\d{3}$"
74	        prompt = "Generate a three-digit number:"
75	
76	        self._run_decode_regex(
77	            regex=pattern,
78	            prompt=prompt,
79	            n=3,
80	        )
81	
82	    def test_regex_generate_phone(self):
83	        pattern = r"^\(\d{3}\) \d{3}-\d{4}$"
84	        prompt = "Generate a phone number:"
85	
86	        self._run_decode_regex(
87	            regex=pattern,
88	            prompt=prompt,
89	            n=3,
90	        )
91	
92	    def test_regex_generate_date(self):
93	        pattern = r"^2024-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-(0[1-9]|[12]\d|3[01])$"
94	        prompt = "Generate a date in YYYY-MM-DD format:"
95	
96	        self._run_decode_regex(
97	            regex=pattern,
98	            prompt=prompt,
99	            n=3,
100	        )

24	class TestJsonSchemaConstraint(unittest.TestCase):
25	    """Test JSON schema constraint generation for tool choices"""
26	
27	    def setUp(self):
28	        """Set up test tools"""
29	        self.tools = [

31	                type="function",
32	                function=Function(
33	                    name="get_weather",
34	                    description="Get weather information",
35	                    parameters={
36	                        "type": "object",
37	                        "properties": {
38	                            "location": {
39	                                "type": "string",
40	                                "description": "Location to get weather for",
41	                            },
42	                            "unit": {
43	                                "type": "string",
44	                                "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
45	                                "description": "Temperature unit",
46	                            },
47	                        },
48	                        "required": ["location"],
49	                    },
50	                ),
51	            ),
52	            Tool(
53	                type="function",
54	                function=Function(
55	                    name="search",
56	                    description="Search for information",
57	                    parameters={
58	                        "type": "object",
59	                        "properties": {
60	                            "query": {
61	                                "type": "string",
62	                                "description": "Search query",
63	                            },
64	                        },

66	                    },

71	    def test_required_tool_choice_schema(self):
72	        """Test schema generation for tool_choice='required'"""
73	        schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, "required")
74	
75	        self.assertIsNotNone(schema)
76	        jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(schema)
77	
78	        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "array")
79	        self.assertEqual(schema["minItems"], 1)
80	        self.assertIn("items", schema)
81	        self.assertIn("anyOf", schema["items"])
82	
83	        # Should have schemas for both tools
84	        self.assertEqual(len(schema["items"]["anyOf"]), 2)
85	
86	        # Check that each tool schema is present
87	        tool_names = [
88	            item["properties"]["name"]["enum"][0] for item in schema["items"]["anyOf"]
89	        ]
90	        self.assertIn("get_weather", tool_names)
91	        self.assertIn("search", tool_names)

213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}
222	
223	        if fmt_type == "tag":
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(
225	                structural_format.get("content")
226	            )
227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233

236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):

261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:

127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
158

190	            verify=self.verify,
191	        )

273	        }
274	        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
275	
276	        input_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
277	        output_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["output_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
278	        normalized_prompt_logprobs = [
279	            compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"])
280	            for r in obj
281	        ]
282	
283	        # Remove extra token if no token healing occurred
284	        for i in range(len(input_token_logprobs)):
285	            healed_token_str = input_token_logprobs[i][0][-1]
286	            if s.text_.endswith(healed_token_str):
287	                healed_token_logprob = input_token_logprobs[i][0][0]
288	                normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] = (
289	                    normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] * len(input_token_logprobs[i])
290	                    - healed_token_logprob
291	                ) / (len(input_token_logprobs[i]) - 1)
292	                input_token_logprobs[i] = input_token_logprobs[i][1:]
293	
294	        # Compute unconditional logprobs if required
295	        if choices_method.requires_unconditional_logprobs:
296	            input_ids = [[el[1] for el in subl] for subl in input_token_logprobs]
297	            data = {
298	                "input_ids": input_ids,
299	                "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0},
300	                "return_logprob": True,
301	            }
302	            obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
303	            unconditional_token_logprobs = [
304	                r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj
305	            ]
306	        else:
307	            unconditional_token_logprobs = None
308	
309	        return choices_method(
310	            choices=choices,
311	            normalized_prompt_logprobs=normalized_prompt_logprobs,
312	            input_token_logprobs=input_token_logprobs,
313	            output_token_logprobs=output_token_logprobs,
314	            unconditional_token_logprobs=unconditional_token_logprobs,
315	        )
316	
317	    def concatenate_and_append(self, src_rids: List[str], dst_rid: str):
318	        res = http_request(
319	            self.base_url + "/concate_and_append_request",
320	            json={"src_rids": src_rids, "dst_rid": dst_rid},
321	            api_key=self.api_key,
322	            verify=self.verify,
323	        )
324	        self._assert_success(res)
325	
326	    def _generate_http_request(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
327	        self._add_images(s, data)
328	        res = http_request(
329	            self.base_url + "/generate",
330	            json=data,
331	            api_key=self.api_key,
332	            verify=self.verify,
333	        )
334	        self._assert_success(res)
335	        return res.json()
336	
337	    def _add_images(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
338	        if s.images_:
339	            assert len(s.images_) == 1, "Only support one image."
340	            data["image_data"] = s.images_[0][1]
341	
342	    def _assert_success(self, res):
343	        if res.status_code != 200:
344	            try:
345	                content = res.json()
346	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
347	                content = res.text
348	            raise RuntimeError(content)
349

25	def create_logit_bias_int(tokenizer):
26	    """Get logit bias for integer numbers."""
27	    int_token_ids = []
28	
29	    tokens = tokenizer._mergeable_ranks
30	    for token, token_id in tokens.items():
31	        s = tokenizer.decode([token_id])
32	        if all([c.isdigit() for c in s]) or s in [" "]:
33	            int_token_ids.append(token_id)
34	            if len(int_token_ids) >= 300:  # OpenAI API limit
35	                break
36	    special_tokens = tokenizer._special_tokens
37	    mask = {t: 100 for t in int_token_ids[:299]}
38	    mask[special_tokens["<|endoftext|>"]] = 100
39	    return mask

1	"""Tracing a program."""
2	
3	import uuid
4	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
5	
6	from sglang.lang.backend.base_backend import BaseBackend
7	from sglang.lang.interpreter import ProgramState, ProgramStateGroup
8	from sglang.lang.ir import (
9	    SglArgument,
10	    SglConstantText,
11	    SglExpr,
12	    SglExprList,
13	    SglFork,
14	    SglGen,
15	    SglGetForkItem,
16	    SglRoleBegin,
17	    SglRoleEnd,
18	    SglSelect,
19	    SglVariable,
20	    SglVarScopeBegin,
21	    SglVarScopeEnd,
22	)
23	
24	
25	class StopTracing(Exception):
26	    pass
27	
28	
29	def extract_prefix_by_tracing(program, backend):
30	    # Create dummy arguments
31	    dummy_arguments = {name: SglArgument(name, None) for name in program.arg_names}
32	    arguments = dummy_arguments
33	    arguments.update(program.bind_arguments)
34	
35	    # Trace
36	    tracer = TracerProgramState(backend, arguments, only_trace_prefix=True)
37	    try:
38	        with TracingScope(tracer):
39	            tracer.ret_value = program.func(tracer, **arguments)
40	    except (StopTracing, TypeError, AttributeError):
41	        # Some exceptions may not be caught
42	        pass
43	
44	    # Run and cache prefix
45	    prefix = ""
46	    for expr in tracer.flatten_nodes():
47	        if isinstance(expr, SglConstantText):
48	            prefix += expr.value
49	        else:
50	            break
51	    return prefix
52	
53	
54	def trace_program(program, arguments, backend):
55	    # Create dummy backend
56	    if backend is None:
57	        backend = BaseBackend()
58	
59	    # Create dummy arguments
60	    dummy_arguments = {
61	        name: SglArgument(name, None)
62	        for name in program.arg_names
63	        if name not in arguments
64	    }
65	    arguments.update(dummy_arguments)
66	    arguments.update(program.bind_arguments)
67	
68	    # Trace
69	    tracer = TracerProgramState(backend, arguments, only_trace_prefix=False)
70	    with TracingScope(tracer):
71	        tracer.ret_value = program.func(tracer, **arguments)
72	    return tracer
73	
74	
75	class TracerProgramState(ProgramState):
76	    def __init__(self, backend, arguments, only_trace_prefix):
77	        self.pid = uuid.uuid4().hex
78	        self.backend = backend
79	        self.arguments: Dict[str, Any] = arguments
80	        self.only_trace_prefix = only_trace_prefix
81	
82	        if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
83	            self.backend = backend.endpoint
84	
85	        self.nodes = []
86	        self.last_node = None
87	        self.variables = {}
88	        self.ret_value = None
89	
90	        # For completion
91	
92	        # For chat
93	        self.messages_ = []
94	        self.cur_role = None
95	        self.chat_template = self.backend.get_chat_template()
96	
97	        # For multi states
98	        self.child_states = []
99	
100	        cur_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
101	        if cur_scope is not None:
102	            cur_scope.add_child_state(self)
103	
104	    ##################################
105	    ########### Public API ###########
106	    ##################################
107	
108	    def fork(self, size: int = 1, position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None):
109	        assert size >= 1
110	
111	        if self.only_trace_prefix:
112	            raise StopTracing()
113	
114	        fork_node = SglFork(size)
115	        fork_node.prev_node = self.last_node
116	
117	        states = [
118	            TracerProgramState(self.backend, self.arguments, self.only_trace_prefix)
119	            for _ in range(size)
120	        ]
121	
122	        for i in range(size):
123	            node = SglGetForkItem(i)
124	            node.prev_node = fork_node
125	            states[i].last_node = node
126	            states[i].variables = dict(self.variables)
127	            states[i].messages_ = list(self.messages_)
128	            states[i].cur_role = self.cur_role
129	            states[i].chat_template = self.chat_template
130	
131	        state_group = ProgramStateGroup(states, self)
132	
133	        return state_group
134	
135	    ##################################
136	    ########## Internal API ##########
137	    ##################################
138	
139	    def _append_node(self, other: SglExpr):
140	        self.nodes.append(other)
141	        other.prev_node = self.last_node
142	        self.last_node = other
143	
144	    def _execute(self, other: SglExpr):
145	        if isinstance(other, str):
146	            other = SglConstantText(other)
147	
148	        other.pid = self.pid
149	
150	        if isinstance(other, SglConstantText):
151	            self._execute_fill(other)
152	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen):
153	            self._execute_gen(other)
154	        elif isinstance(other, SglSelect):
155	            self._execute_select(other)
156	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
157	            for x in other.expr_list:
158	                self._execute(x)
159	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleBegin):
160	            self._execute_role_begin(other)
161	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleEnd):
162	            self._execute_role_end(other)
163	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeBegin):
164	            self._execute_var_scope_begin(other)
165	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeEnd):
166	            self._execute_var_scope_end(other)
167	        else:
168	            if self.only_trace_prefix:
169	                raise StopTracing()
170	            else:
171	                self._append_node(other)
172	
173	        return self
174	
175	    def __iadd__(self, other):
176	        self._execute(other)
177	        return self
178	
179	    def _execute_fill(self, expr: SglConstantText):

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document:

## Comprehensive Analysis: Output Constraints in SGLang

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's how output constraints are implemented:

### 1. **Directory Structure for Constraint Implementation**

The constraint-related code is organized in these key locations:

```
python/sglang/
├── lang/
│   ├── api.py              # Public API definitions (gen, select, etc.)
│   ├── ir.py               # Intermediate representation (SglGen, SglSelect, SglSamplingParams)
│   ├── choices.py          # Choice constraint sampling methods
│   └── interpreter.py      # Execution logic
├── srt/
│   ├── constrained/        # Grammar backend implementations
│   │   ├── base_grammar_backend.py
│   │   ├── grammar_manager.py
│   │   ├── outlines_backend.py
│   │   ├── xgrammar_backend.py
│   │   ├── llguidance_backend.py
│   │   └── utils.py
│   └── sampling/
│       └── sampling_params.py
└── test/
    └── kits/
        ├── json_constrained_kit.py
        ├── regex_constrained_kit.py
        └── ebnf_constrained_kit.py
```

### 2. **Core Constraint Types**

SGLang supports **5 main constraint types**:

1. **`regex`** - Regular expression constraints
2. **`json_schema`** - JSON schema constraints
3. **`ebnf`** - Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar constraints
4. **`structural_tag`** - Structural markup constraints (for function calls, XML output)
5. **`choices`** - Discrete choice selection

### 3. **Class/Function Signatures and Patterns**

#### **A. Main IR Classes (in `lang/ir.py`)**

```python
class SglSamplingParams:
    def __init__(self, ...
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        dtype: Optional[str] = None,  # Converted to regex
        ...
    )

class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        ...
    )

class SglSelect(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self,
        name: str,
        choices: List[str],
        temperature: float,
        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
    )
```

#### **B. Public API Functions (in `lang/api.py`)**

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,  # Triggers SglSelect
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    ...
) -> Union[SglGen, SglSelect]:
    # Routes to SglSelect if choices provided, else SglGen
    
def select(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod = token_length_normalized,
) -> SglSelect:
    # Direct choice selection API
```

#### **C. Choice Sampling Methods (in `lang/choices.py`)**

Abstract base class with 3 concrete implementations:

```python
class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
    @property
    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
        return False
    
    @abstractmethod
    def __call__(
        self,
        choices: List[str],
        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
        ...

# Implementations:
class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod)      # Default
class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod)
class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod)
```

### 4. **Grammar Backend Architecture**

The constraint implementation uses a **pluggable backend pattern**:

#### **A. Base Class Hierarchy (in `base_grammar_backend.py`)**

```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    """Base class for grammar objects"""
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        """Accept token and update grammar state"""
    
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Create mask tensor for allowed tokens"""
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        """Fill vocab mask with constrained tokens"""
    
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        """Apply mask to logits"""
    
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
        """Optimization: jump ahead multiple tokens"""
    
    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper) -> Tuple[str, int]:
        """Process jumped tokens"""

class BaseGrammarBackend:
    """Backend for compiling and caching grammar objects"""
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    
    def get_cached_or_future_value(self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool)
    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject)
```

#### **B. Concrete Implementations**

**Outlines Backend** (`outlines_backend.py`):
- Uses `outlines` library's regex/JSON support
- Class: `OutlinesGrammar`, `OutlinesGrammarBackend`
- Supports: `json_schema`, `regex`

**XGrammar Backend** (`xgrammar_backend.py`):
- Uses `xgrammar` library (most feature-complete)
- Class: `XGrammarGrammar`, `XGrammarGrammarBackend`
- Supports: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- Features: Token bitmask operations, jump-forward optimization

**LLGuidance Backend** (`llguidance_backend.py`):
- Uses `llguidance` library
- Class: `GuidanceGrammar`, `GuidanceBackend`
- Supports: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`

### 5. **Constraint Flow and Execution**

#### **Flow Diagram:**

```
User Code: sgl.gen(regex="...", json_schema="...")
    ↓
api.gen() [lang/api.py]
    ↓
SglGen or SglSelect IR object created
    ↓
Interpreter executes [lang/interpreter.py]
    ↓
GrammarManager processes request [srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py]
    - Extracts constraint key: ("json" | "regex" | "ebnf" | "structural_tag", value)
    - Gets grammar object from backend (cached or async compiled)
    ↓
Backend compiles grammar [base_grammar_backend.py → dispatch_*]
    - Returns BaseGrammarObject with vocab_mask capabilities
    ↓
Sampling uses vocab_mask to restrict logits
    - fill_vocab_mask() creates allowed tokens mask
    - apply_vocab_mask() constrains logits
```

#### **Key Class: GrammarManager** (in `srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`)

```python
class GrammarManager:
    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        """Create grammar objects for requests with constraints"""
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(key, require_reasoning)
        req.grammar = value
        return add_to_grammar_queue

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
        """Poll for compiled grammars and sync across distributed ranks"""
```

### 6. **Constraint Registration and Exports**

#### **Public Exports** (in `sglang/__init__.py`)

The library exports constraint-related APIs:
- `gen()` - Main generation function with constraint support
- `gen_int()`, `gen_string()` - Specialized generators with `dtype`
- `select()` - Choice selection function
- Choice sampling methods: `token_length_normalized`, `greedy_token_selection`, `unconditional_likelihood_normalized`

#### **Backend Registration** (in `base_grammar_backend.py`)

```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
    # Priority: custom backends > built-in (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance, none)
```

### 7. **Sampling Parameters Integration**

The `SamplingParams` class (in `srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`) includes:

```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(self,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ...
    )
```

These parameters are:
- Passed from frontend through `SglGen.sampling_params`
- Converted to server format via `SglSamplingParams.to_srt_kwargs()`
- Used by GrammarManager to create constraint keys

### 8. **Special Features**

- **Dtype Conversion**: `dtype` parameter auto-converts to regex patterns
  ```python
  REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
  REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
  REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
  REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
  ```

- **Jump-Forward Optimization**: Backends can skip multiple tokens at once if the grammar allows it

- **Async Compilation**: Grammar compilation happens asynchronously with polling and timeout handling

- **Distributed Sync**: Grammar objects are synchronized across distributed ranks (AllGather)

### 9. **Test Organization**

Constraint tests are organized in mixins for easy backend testing:

```
test/kits/
├── json_constrained_kit.py    → TestJSONConstrainedMixin
├── regex_constrained_kit.py    → TestRegexConstrainedMixin
├── ebnf_constrained_kit.py     → TestEBNFConstrainedMixin
```

Each backend can inherit these mixins to run the same tests with different backends.

---

## Summary

SGLang implements constraints through a **modular, pluggable architecture** where:
1. **IR Layer** defines constraint representation (SglGen, SglSelect, SamplingParams)
2. **Interpreter Layer** routes constraints through the grammar manager
3. **Backend Layer** compiles constraints into finite-state automata/FSMs
4. **Sampling Layer** uses grammar objects to restrict token probabilities via vocab masks
5. **Choice Layer** provides multiple strategies for discrete selection

The implementation separates concerns cleanly, allowing different backends (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance) to implement constraint compilation independently while sharing the same interface and sampling logic.

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document:

## Comprehensive Analysis: Output Constraints in SGLang

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's how output constraints are implemented:

### 1. **Directory Structure for Constraint Implementation**

The constraint-related code is organized in these key locations:

```
python/sglang/
├── lang/
│   ├── api.py              # Public API definitions (gen, select, etc.)
│   ├── ir.py               # Intermediate representation (SglGen, SglSelect, SglSamplingParams)
│   ├── choices.py          # Choice constraint sampling methods
│   └── interpreter.py      # Execution logic
├── srt/
│   ├── constrained/        # Grammar backend implementations
│   │   ├── base_grammar_backend.py
│   │   ├── grammar_manager.py
│   │   ├── outlines_backend.py
│   │   ├── xgrammar_backend.py
│   │   ├── llguidance_backend.py
│   │   └── utils.py
│   └── sampling/
│       └── sampling_params.py
└── test/
    └── kits/
        ├── json_constrained_kit.py
        ├── regex_constrained_kit.py
        └── ebnf_constrained_kit.py
```

### 2. **Core Constraint Types**

SGLang supports **5 main constraint types**:

1. **`regex`** - Regular expression constraints
2. **`json_schema`** - JSON schema constraints
3. **`ebnf`** - Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar constraints
4. **`structural_tag`** - Structural markup constraints (for function calls, XML output)
5. **`choices`** - Discrete choice selection

### 3. **Class/Function Signatures and Patterns**

#### **A. Main IR Classes (in `lang/ir.py`)**

```python
class SglSamplingParams:
    def __init__(self, ...
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        dtype: Optional[str] = None,  # Converted to regex
        ...
    )

class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        ...
    )

class SglSelect(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self,
        name: str,
        choices: List[str],
        temperature: float,
        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
    )
```

#### **B. Public API Functions (in `lang/api.py`)**

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,  # Triggers SglSelect
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    ...
) -> Union[SglGen, SglSelect]:
    # Routes to SglSelect if choices provided, else SglGen
    
def select(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod = token_length_normalized,
) -> SglSelect:
    # Direct choice selection API
```

#### **C. Choice Sampling Methods (in `lang/choices.py`)**

Abstract base class with 3 concrete implementations:

```python
class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
    @property
    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
        return False
    
    @abstractmethod
    def __call__(
        self,
        choices: List[str],
        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
        ...

# Implementations:
class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod)      # Default
class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod)
class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod)
```

### 4. **Grammar Backend Architecture**

The constraint implementation uses a **pluggable backend pattern**:

#### **A. Base Class Hierarchy (in `base_grammar_backend.py`)**

```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    """Base class for grammar objects"""
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        """Accept token and update grammar state"""
    
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Create mask tensor for allowed tokens"""
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        """Fill vocab mask with constrained tokens"""
    
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        """Apply mask to logits"""
    
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
        """Optimization: jump ahead multiple tokens"""
    
    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper) -> Tuple[str, int]:
        """Process jumped tokens"""

class BaseGrammarBackend:
    """Backend for compiling and caching grammar objects"""
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
    
    def get_cached_or_future_value(self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool)
    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject)
```

#### **B. Concrete Implementations**

**Outlines Backend** (`outlines_backend.py`):
- Uses `outlines` library's regex/JSON support
- Class: `OutlinesGrammar`, `OutlinesGrammarBackend`
- Supports: `json_schema`, `regex`

**XGrammar Backend** (`xgrammar_backend.py`):
- Uses `xgrammar` library (most feature-complete)
- Class: `XGrammarGrammar`, `XGrammarGrammarBackend`
- Supports: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- Features: Token bitmask operations, jump-forward optimization

**LLGuidance Backend** (`llguidance_backend.py`):
- Uses `llguidance` library
- Class: `GuidanceGrammar`, `GuidanceBackend`
- Supports: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`

### 5. **Constraint Flow and Execution**

#### **Flow Diagram:**

```
User Code: sgl.gen(regex="...", json_schema="...")
    ↓
api.gen() [lang/api.py]
    ↓
SglGen or SglSelect IR object created
    ↓
Interpreter executes [lang/interpreter.py]
    ↓
GrammarManager processes request [srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py]
    - Extracts constraint key: ("json" | "regex" | "ebnf" | "structural_tag", value)
    - Gets grammar object from backend (cached or async compiled)
    ↓
Backend compiles grammar [base_grammar_backend.py → dispatch_*]
    - Returns BaseGrammarObject with vocab_mask capabilities
    ↓
Sampling uses vocab_mask to restrict logits
    - fill_vocab_mask() creates allowed tokens mask
    - apply_vocab_mask() constrains logits
```

#### **Key Class: GrammarManager** (in `srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`)

```python
class GrammarManager:
    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        """Create grammar objects for requests with constraints"""
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(key, require_reasoning)
        req.grammar = value
        return add_to_grammar_queue

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
        """Poll for compiled grammars and sync across distributed ranks"""
```

### 6. **Constraint Registration and Exports**

#### **Public Exports** (in `sglang/__init__.py`)

The library exports constraint-related APIs:
- `gen()` - Main generation function with constraint support
- `gen_int()`, `gen_string()` - Specialized generators with `dtype`
- `select()` - Choice selection function
- Choice sampling methods: `token_length_normalized`, `greedy_token_selection`, `unconditional_likelihood_normalized`

#### **Backend Registration** (in `base_grammar_backend.py`)

```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
    # Priority: custom backends > built-in (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance, none)
```

### 7. **Sampling Parameters Integration**

The `SamplingParams` class (in `srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`) includes:

```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(self,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ...
    )
```

These parameters are:
- Passed from frontend through `SglGen.sampling_params`
- Converted to server format via `SglSamplingParams.to_srt_kwargs()`
- Used by GrammarManager to create constraint keys

### 8. **Special Features**

- **Dtype Conversion**: `dtype` parameter auto-converts to regex patterns
  ```python
  REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
  REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
  REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
  REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
  ```

- **Jump-Forward Optimization**: Backends can skip multiple tokens at once if the grammar allows it

- **Async Compilation**: Grammar compilation happens asynchronously with polling and timeout handling

- **Distributed Sync**: Grammar objects are synchronized across distributed ranks (AllGather)

### 9. **Test Organization**

Constraint tests are organized in mixins for easy backend testing:

```
test/kits/
├── json_constrained_kit.py    → TestJSONConstrainedMixin
├── regex_constrained_kit.py    → TestRegexConstrainedMixin
├── ebnf_constrained_kit.py     → TestEBNFConstrainedMixin
```

Each backend can inherit these mixins to run the same tests with different backends.

---

## Summary

SGLang implements constraints through a **modular, pluggable architecture** where:
1. **IR Layer** defines constraint representation (SglGen, SglSelect, SamplingParams)
2. **Interpreter Layer** routes constraints through the grammar manager
3. **Backend Layer** compiles constraints into finite-state automata/FSMs
4. **Sampling Layer** uses grammar objects to restrict token probabilities via vocab masks
5. **Choice Layer** provides multiple strategies for discrete selection

The implementation separates concerns cleanly, allowing different backends (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance) to implement constraint compilation independently while sharing the same interface and sampling logic.

14	"""Constrained decoding with outlines backend."""
15	
16	import json
17	import logging
18	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
19	
20	import interegular
21	import torch
22	from outlines.fsm.guide import RegexGuide
23	from outlines.models.transformers import TransformerTokenizer
24	from pydantic import BaseModel
25	
26	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
27	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
28	    BaseGrammarBackend,
29	    BaseGrammarObject,
30	)
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_jump_forward import OutlinesJumpForwardMap
32	
33	try:
34	    from outlines.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
35	except ImportError:
36	    from outlines_core.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
37	
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state
112	
113	
114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)
191

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a summary document with my findings:

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here is a comprehensive summary of how the high-level API and program constructs work, with special focus on constraints:

## SGLang High-Level API and Program Constructs: Comprehensive Overview

### 1. Directory Structure

The SGLang codebase is organized as follows:
- **`python/sglang/lang/`** - Frontend language API and execution layer:
  - `api.py` - Public APIs for `gen()`, `select()`, roles, etc.
  - `ir.py` - Intermediate representation (IR) dataclasses
  - `interpreter.py` - Execution engine (StreamExecutor, ProgramState)
  - `tracer.py` - Program tracing for prefix caching
  - `choices.py` - Choice selection algorithms
  - `backend/` - Backend implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, RuntimeEndpoint)

- **`python/sglang/srt/constrained/`** - Constraint compilation and enforcement:
  - `base_grammar_backend.py` - Base class for grammar-based constraints
  - `outlines_backend.py` - Regex/JSON schema constraints via Outlines
  - `xgrammar_backend.py` - Advanced grammar backend

### 2. Generation Primitives: `gen()` and `select()`

#### **`gen()` Function** (in `api.py`, lines 75-139)
```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
```

**Key behavior:**
- If `choices` is provided, returns an `SglSelect` object instead of `SglGen`
- Otherwise, creates an `SglGen` IR node with all parameters
- Validates regex syntax at API level (line 112-115)

#### **`select()` Function** (in `api.py`, lines 236-243)
```python
def select(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod = token_length_normalized,
):
```

**Key behavior:**
- Wrapper that directly calls `SglSelect()` IR node
- `choices` is required (assert at line 242)
- Uses token-length-normalized scoring by default

### 3. Constraint Parameters and Data Flow

#### **SglSamplingParams Dataclass** (in `ir.py`, lines 18-138)
The core constraint container with these constraint-related fields:

```python
@dataclasses.dataclass
class SglSamplingParams:
    # Regular sampling parameters (included in backend conversion)
    max_new_tokens: int = 128
    min_new_tokens: int = 0
    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = -1
    # ... other sampling params ...
    
    # Constraint parameters (NOT included in backend conversions)
    dtype: Optional[str] = None          # Type hint: int, float, str, bool
    regex: Optional[str] = None          # Regex pattern constraint
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None    # JSON schema constraint
```

**Important:** The `dtype` and `regex` fields are explicitly NOT included in `to_openai_kwargs()`, `to_anthropic_kwargs()`, etc. - only the SRT backend uses them (via `to_srt_kwargs()`).

#### **SglGen IR Node** (in `ir.py`, lines 451-503)
```python
class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        max_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        # ... all sampling parameters ...
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        self.name = name
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            # ... populated with all parameters including constraints
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )
```

#### **SglSelect IR Node** (in `ir.py`, lines 533-549)
```python
class SglSelect(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: str,
        choices: List[str],
        temperature: float,
        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
    ):
        self.name = name
        self.choices = choices
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.choices_method = choices_method
```

### 4. How Constraints Flow Through the Execution Pipeline

#### **Step 1: Program Definition**
User writes:
```python
@sgl.function
def my_program(s):
    s += "Generate JSON: "
    s += sgl.gen("result", max_tokens=256, regex=r"\{.*\}")
```

#### **Step 2: Tracing Phase** (in `tracer.py`)
When the function is traced, `SglGen` and `SglSelect` IR nodes are created and stored in the trace.

#### **Step 3: Execution - StreamExecutor** (in `interpreter.py`, lines 250-822)
The `StreamExecutor` class:
- Stores `default_sampling_para` (SglSamplingParams from `run()` call)
- Has a `_resolve_sampling_params()` method (lines 775-822) that:
  - Starts with `default_sampling_para` as the base
  - Merges override values from the individual `SglGen.sampling_params`
  - Extends stop tokens based on chat template
  - Returns the merged parameters

#### **Step 4: Constraint Resolution at Execution**
When `_execute_gen()` is called (line 569):
1. Calls `_resolve_sampling_params(expr.sampling_params)` to merge constraints
2. Resolves `dtype` to `regex` via `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` (in RuntimeEndpoint, lines 127-157)
3. Passes merged `SglSamplingParams` to backend's `generate()` method

#### **Step 5: Backend-Specific Constraint Handling**

**RuntimeEndpoint** (`runtime_endpoint.py`):
- `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` (lines 127-157): Converts dtype hint to regex pattern:
  - `int` → `REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"`
  - `float` → `REGEX_FLOAT`
  - `str` → `REGEX_STR`
  - `bool` → `REGEX_BOOL`
- Calls `to_srt_kwargs()` which includes `regex` and `json_schema`

**OpenAI Backend** (`openai.py`):
- `to_openai_kwargs()` **drops** `regex` and `json_schema` (not supported)
- Issues warning if regex is used with OpenAI

**Anthropic Backend** (`anthropic.py`):
- Similar to OpenAI - constraints not supported

### 5. Constraint Selection Mechanisms

#### **Choice Selection Methods** (in `choices.py`)
Three built-in implementations of `ChoicesSamplingMethod`:

1. **TokenLengthNormalized** (default)
   - Selects choice with highest normalized prompt logprob
   - Divides total logprob by number of tokens

2. **GreedyTokenSelection**
   - Selects greedily token-by-token
   - Handles overlapping options with average logprob extension

3. **UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized**
   - Normalizes by unconditional token logprobs
   - Requires `requires_unconditional_logprobs=True` flag

#### **Select Execution** (in `interpreter.py`, lines 623-634)
```python
def _execute_select(self, expr: SglSelect):
    choices_decision = self.backend.select(
        self, expr.choices, expr.temperature, expr.choices_method
    )
    if expr.name is not None:
        name = expr.name
        self.variables[name] = choices_decision.decision
        self.meta_info[name] = choices_decision.meta_info
        self.variable_event[name].set()
    self.text_ += choices_decision.decision
```

### 6. Constraint Compilation Layer (SRT Backend)

The `python/sglang/srt/constrained/` directory contains the actual constraint enforcement:

**BaseGrammarBackend** (`base_grammar_backend.py`):
- Abstract class for constraint compilers
- Caches compiled grammars by `(dispatch_type, key_string)`
- Dispatches to specific implementations via `dispatch_regex()`, `dispatch_json()`

**OutlinesGrammarBackend** (`outlines_backend.py`):
- Compiles regex → `RegexGuide` (FSM from Outlines library)
- Compiles JSON schema → regex via `build_regex_from_schema()`
- Creates `OutlinesGrammar` objects that track FSM state

**BaseGrammarObject** (constraint enforcement interface):
- `accept_token(token)` - Validates token against grammar FSM
- `allocate_vocab_mask()` - Creates vocabulary mask tensors
- `fill_vocab_mask()` - Sets allowed tokens for current state
- `apply_vocab_mask()` - Masks logits with -inf for disallowed tokens

### 7. Test Examples Demonstrating Constraint Usage

**regex constraint** (from `readme_examples.py`, lines 43-49):
```python
@sgl.function
def regular_expression_gen(s):
    s += "Q: What is the IP address of the Google DNS servers?\n"
    s += "A: " + sgl.gen(
        "answer",
        temperature=0,
        regex=r"((25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|[01]?\d\d?).){3}(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|[01]?\d\d?)",
    )
```

**choices constraint** (from `readme_examples.py`, lines 10-22):
```python
@sgl.function
def tool_use(s, question):
    s += "To answer this question: " + question + ". "
    s += (
        "I need to use a "
        + sgl.gen("tool", choices=["calculator", "search engine"])
        + ". "
    )
```

**json_schema constraint** (from `json_decode.py`, lines 64-72):
```python
@sgl.function
def pydantic_wizard_gen(s):
    s += "Give me a description about a wizard in the JSON format.\n"
    s += sgl.gen(
        "character",
        max_tokens=128,
        temperature=0,
        regex=build_regex_from_object(Wizard),  # Pydantic model to regex
    )
```

### 8. API Parameter Summary

**Key constraint parameters accepted by `gen()`:**
- `choices: Optional[List[str]]` - Discrete choice selection
- `regex: Optional[str]` - Regular expression constraint
- `json_schema: Optional[str]` - JSON schema constraint
- `dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]]` - Type hint (int, float, str, bool)
- `temperature: Optional[float]` - Sampling temperature
- `max_tokens: Optional[int]` - Max generation length
- `min_tokens: Optional[int]` - Min generation length
- `stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]]` - Stop strings
- `stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]]` - Stop patterns

**Note:** When `choices` is provided, `gen()` returns `SglSelect` instead of `SglGen`, making the choices constraint take precedence.

This architecture cleanly separates:
1. **Frontend API** (`api.py`) - User-facing constraint specification
2. **IR Layer** (`ir.py`) - Constraint representation in execution graph
3. **Interpreter** (`interpreter.py`) - Constraint merging and resolution
4. **Backend Layer** - Backend-specific constraint handling
5. **Constraint Engine** (`srt/constrained/`) - Grammar compilation and FSM enforcement

```python
        + ". "
    )
```

**json_schema constraint** (from `json_decode.py`, lines 64-72):
```python
@sgl.function
def pydantic_wizard_gen(s):
    s += "Give me a description about a wizard in the JSON format.\n"
    s += sgl.gen(
        "character",
        max_tokens=128,
        temperature=0,
        regex=build_regex_from_object(Wizard),  # Pydantic model to regex
    )
```

### 8. API Parameter Summary

**Key constraint parameters accepted by `gen()`:**
- `choices: Optional[List[str]]` - Discrete choice selection
- `regex: Optional[str]` - Regular expression constraint
- `json_schema: Optional[str]` - JSON schema constraint
- `dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]]` - Type hint (int, float, str, bool)
- `temperature: Optional[float]` - Sampling temperature
- `max_tokens: Optional[int]` - Max generation length
- `min_tokens: Optional[int]` - Min generation length
- `stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]]` - Stop strings
- `stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]]` - Stop patterns

**Note:** When `choices` is provided, `gen()` returns `SglSelect` instead of `SglGen`, making the choices constraint take precedence.

This architecture cleanly separates:
1. **Frontend API** (`api.py`) - User-facing constraint specification
2. **IR Layer** (`ir.py`) - Constraint representation in execution graph
3. **Interpreter** (`interpreter.py`) - Constraint merging and resolution
4. **Backend Layer** - Backend-specific constraint handling
5. **Constraint Engine** (`srt/constrained/`) - Grammar compilation and FSM enforcement

1	"""The intermediate representation."""
2	
3	import dataclasses
4	import inspect
5	import warnings
6	from typing import List, Optional, Union
7	
8	from sglang.global_config import global_config
9	from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod
10	
11	REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
12	REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
13	REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
14	REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg
15	
16	
17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,
57	            self.return_logprob,
58	            self.logprob_start_len,
59	            self.top_logprobs_num,
60	            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
61	            self.json_schema,
62	        )
63	
64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }
78	
79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }
92	
93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
103	            ),
104	            "temperature": self.temperature,
105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }
108	
109	    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
110	        if self.regex is not None:
111	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
112	        return {
113	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
114	            "stop": self.stop or None,
115	            "temperature": self.temperature,
116	            "top_p": self.top_p,
117	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
118	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
119	        }
120
